Params: {3: 'Dark ink floods the space of her M.I.N.D., washing away every last construct until only a quiet, empty void remains.'}

{1: '1', 3: 'In the stillness, countless bubbles—pure and translucent as frozen dew—float gently in the air. Beyond that dreamlike expanse, you finally see her: the Construct girl standing in the distance.'}
{3: 'Her lashes tremble as she opens her eyes.'}

{3: 'Before her eyes lies a field of crystalline spheres, each one gleaming like a kaleidoscope. Their transparent walls ripple with shifting rainbows, so pure that even nature could never have shaped them.'}
{3: 'Inside each bubble, turning to a silent rhythm, flicker fragments of memory:'}
{3: 'Pink hair flying in the wind, fingertips meeting above a swaying sunroof, a tired face resting under moonlight, rainbows, sunsets, a seaside walkway brushing against flowing waves...'}
{3: 'Joyful memories that should have built a bridge to happiness now spin and collide, stacking into walls of rejection.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '(I\'d rather believe I was just a simulation than admit my feelings went out of control...)', 4: '1', 18: '100014349'}
{3: 'In the destiny Teddy built for herself, love was an unwanted variable—the only madness capable of breaking her fortress.'}
{3: 'Love would make her incomplete, so the part that longed to be loved could only be a false entity, never her true self.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: '...It\'s stable now. They probably won\'t appear again.', 4: '1', 18: '100014350'}
{3: 'When Teddy makes a promise, you believe her.'}
{3: 'But you already know the truth. The virus, the wandering, the conflict between her personalities—they all came from the heart she refused to face.'}
{3: 'Emotions dammed up in silence will one day break free. Only when she finds her own river that runs to the sea will she finally be free.'}

{1: '21', 2: '<color=#000000ff><size=25>The quantum leap has already begun,\neven if it means shattering the order we\'ve always known.</size></color>', 3: '1210', 4: '288', 6: '1', 7: '1', 8: '1.5', 9: '3', 10: '1'}
{3: 'The quantum leap has already begun, even if it means shattering the order we\'ve always known.'}

{1: '22', 2: '<color=#000000ff><size=25>It means reinterpreting the world we thought we understood.\nIt means a future that\'s no longer steady or certain.</size></color>', 3: '1210', 4: '146', 6: '1', 7: '1', 8: '1.5', 9: '3', 10: '1'}
{3: 'It means reinterpreting the world we thought we understood. It means a future that\'s no longer steady or certain.'}
{3: 'Screw it. I\'m going beyond the boundary.'}
{1: '24', 2: '<color=#000000ff><size=25>Into the new world beyond the broken fortress.</size></color>', 3: '1210', 4: '-53', 6: '1', 7: '1', 8: '1', 9: '3', 10: '1'}
{3: 'Into the new world beyond the broken fortress.'}
{2: 'Do you remember why you chose to leave that home?', 3: '137'}
{3: 'That moment back then mirrors this one now.'}
{3: 'Planck time marks the very start of the universe\'s evolution. At that scale, spacetime itself stops behaving as we know it. It\'s the very boundary of physics.'}
{1: '31', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=40>Within that boundary, the world can be known.</size></color>', 3: '-500', 4: '50', 9: '1', 11: '1'}
{3: 'Within that boundary, the world can be known. Beyond it, it\'s unknowable, undefined, untouchable.'}
{1: 'Christina said, "Screw it, I\'m going beyond the boundary."', 3: '45'}
{1: '1', 3: 'Christina said, "Screw it, I\'m going beyond the boundary."', 4: '1'}
{1: '1', 3: 'And so, Teddy becomes the first bear to break free from her cage.', 4: '1'}
{2: 'Christina understood. You do too, don\'t you?', 3: '195'}
{3: 'That moment back then mirrors this one now.'}
{3: 'From the very moment doubt was born, the door had already opened. No one could stop what followed.'}
{3: 'When something challenges the order inside your heart, that challenge itself is the answer.'}
{2: 'Teddy, take a step back. No... just one step will do.', 3: '207'}
{3: 'The pink-haired Construct looks confused, but she still obeys and takes a small step back.'}
{3: 'You check your weapon, lift it steadily, and tap a few quick commands. The crystalline bubbles burst in silence, vanishing like sighs, delicate and fleeting.'}

{3: 'Countless fragments of color scatter through the air like stars and aurora shattering together, tracing brilliant arcs across the sky. The wall of bubbles breaks open, leaving a glowing path through its center.'}
{3: 'Like a door thrown open, light spills across the ground, and at the end of that radiant path stands Teddy.'}
{3: 'You walk through the opening and stop before her.'}
{2: 'You know, Teddy, you can be really cute when you get caught up in these little dilemmas.', 3: '232', 4: 'This journey... it\'s been quite something.', 5: '232', 6: '...', 7: '232'}
{3: 'Because you told her to step back, the falling shards of starlight don\'t touch her.'}
{2: 'See? I\'m kinder than you think. Even here in your M.I.N.D., I didn\'t want to mess up your dress.', 3: '234', 4: 'Now then... allow me to fix it.', 5: '234'}
{3: 'She lets out a small laugh that sounds like a sigh.'}
{3: 'Still, because of that half-step difference, a few traces of color land softly on her cheek.'}
{3: 'The stardust that brushed her cheek turns beneath your touch into a bridge of light.'}
{3: 'Love doesn\'t make us incomplete. Love simply makes us aware of our incompleteness, and that\'s why we...'}
{3: 'Her forehead rests against your shoulder as a quiet, relieved laugh escapes her lips.'}
{3: 'Like a fledgling that\'s finally found shelter from the storm. Now, at last, someone has heard her silent cry for help.'}
{3: 'Reason has saved this world countless times.'}
{3: 'But just as many times, emotion has moved people to impossible acts—the desperate, selfless choices that logic could never justify.'}
{3: 'Humanity can never be measured by reason alone. We\'ll always give our lives to those irrational moments that make us human.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Teddy', 3: 'That girl... what did she ask you? I mean Chris, the other me.', 4: '1', 18: '100014359'}
{3: 'Her forehead rests against your shoulder as a quiet, relieved laugh escapes her lips.'}
{3: 'Like a fledgling that\'s finally found shelter from the storm. Now, at last, someone has heard her silent cry for help.'}
{3: 'Reason has saved this world countless times.'}
{3: 'But just as many times, emotion has moved people to impossible acts—the desperate, selfless choices that logic could never justify.'}
{3: 'Humanity can never be measured by reason alone. We\'ll always give our lives to those irrational moments that make us human.'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Teddy', 3: 'That girl... what did she ask you? I mean Chris, the other me.', 4: '1', 18: '100014359'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Chris', 3: 'Can I just ask you one question, 【kuroname】?', 4: '1', 18: '100014360'}
{2: 'Chris', 3: 'If someday I lose everything I\'m proud of, and become fragile, blind, ordinary...', 4: '1', 18: '100014361'}
{2: 'Chris', 3: 'Would you still choose to walk beside me?', 4: '1', 18: '100014362'}
{2: 'That day will never come.', 3: '299', 4: 'Of course I would.', 5: '301'}
{3: 'You\'d never stand by and watch her lose everything. And Teddy... she\'ll always have the strength and courage to save herself.'}
{3: 'No matter what happens, she\'s still the Teddy who\'s walked beside you until now—your one and only Teddy. That will never change.'}

{3: 'You\'re not just allies who trust each other completely. You\'re comrades who fight side by side, bound by the same ideals. From the moment you stood shoulder to shoulder, it was already decided—you\'d keep supporting each other until the very end.'}
{2: 'Wait... you don\'t need me to explain?', 3: '308'}
{2: 'Chris', 3: 'Of course not. You said it, and that\'s enough. Whatever you say, I\'ll believe.', 4: '1', 18: '100014364'}
{2: 'Chris', 3: 'No reasons, no logic required. Isn\'t that what "love" is?', 4: '1', 18: '100014365'}
{3: 'A faint tickle at the corner of Teddy\'s eyes makes her think tears are forming. When she reaches up, she realizes it\'s your hand, gently wiping away the traces of her tears.'}
{2: 'Teddy, you\'re not a tool built to save this world.', 3: '321'}
{2: 'Everything I\'ve done, it\'s so you, and everyone like you, can find happiness.', 3: '323'}
{2: 'Follow your heart, wherever it takes you. Even if it\'s madness, that\'s fine.', 3: '325'}

{2: 'And I\'ll always be where you\'re heading.', 3: '327', 4: 'I\'ll always be by your side, moving toward that goal with you.', 5: '327', 6: 'We\'ll always be each other\'s strength and courage.', 7: '327'}
{3: 'In your arms, Teddy finally lifts her head.'}
{3: 'The weightless soul slowly steadies itself. Outside, the sun has already tilted westward.'}
{2: 'There\'s only one afternoon left of the holiday. Where do you want to go?', 3: '342'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Hm? But the Constellia music festival\'s been cancelled... Is there anywhere else you want to go?', 4: '1', 18: '100014368'}
{3: 'You glance up at the sun, check the date, time, and weather on your terminal, then take her hand and lead her toward the transport vehicle.'}
{2: 'There\'s a place we can reach just in time if we leave now.', 3: '353'}
{3: 'Ignoring the pre-set routes, you drive along roads untouched by the typhoon, leaving behind the heavy clouds and rain.'}

{3: 'After hours of racing through wind and dust like fugitives, the transport vehicle leaves behind the monotonous roads and barren plains, finally stopping beside an abandoned observation deck perched alone on a high cliff.'}
{2: 'Do you remember the photo from the road guide?', 3: '389'}
{2: '<size=27>The notes had said that whenever a typhoon made landfall in Constellia, a rare sunset would appear here, thousands of miles away.</size>', 3: '410'}
{3: 'You guide Teddy along the worn path. Rusted signs lean sideways, whispering of years of solitude.'}
{3: 'When her gaze drifts past your shoulder toward the open sky, the clouds seem to cross the distance between reality and dream, wrapping her gently in their light.'}
{3: 'The sky glows vast and magnificent, its purple and pink hues soft and deep, just like her eyes.'}

{3: 'The sky that felt so distant at noon now draws close at dusk. The clouds fold inward like tired birds returning to their nests, embracing you both. The world feels like a dream spilled across the horizon—rich, tender, and a little unreal.'}
{3: 'Across the gold-threaded sky, pink and purple blur like watercolor carried by the wind, flowing endlessly until they sink into the dark silhouettes of distant mountains.'}

{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'It\'s caused by Rayleigh scattering under the influence of the northern updraft. The particles in the clouds here must be different. After Mie scattering... that\'s what creates this unique color.', 18: '100014369'}
{3: 'As pink mingles with purple, emotion chases reason. When boundaries fade, both genius and ordinary souls find a brief rest in that twilight.'}
{3: 'Leading Teddy to the railing of the viewing platform, you offer words of praise.'}
{2: 'That\'s the Teddy I know.', 3: '511', 4: 'Is there anything you don\'t know?', 5: '511'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'It\'s just basic knowledge retrieval. If you\'re going to praise me for that... you\'ll be doing it all the time.', 18: '100014370'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Besides, I have high standards for compliments. You\'ll have to try harder next time.', 18: '100014371'}
{3: 'Teddy looks at you, who are smiling.'}
{2: 'Hmm, sounds like the Teddy I remember.', 3: '518'}
{3: 'Her odd behavior during the Cyber Counterpunch interference flashes vividly through her M.I.N.D.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Ugh... you saw that side of me. No choice now. I\'ll have to silence you!', 18: '100014373'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'If you want to live, you\'ll have to grant me one request. No, three requests.', 18: '100014374'}
{2: 'Hold on, how did we jump from three to ten thousand?', 3: '542'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Because someone\'s going to be stuck with me for a very long time.', 18: '100014376'}
{3: 'Without warning, she turns and leans back against the railing, her pink-purple eyes glowing like sunset clouds as she looks straight at you.'}
{3: 'She pauses, searching for the right words to describe everything before her.'}
{3: 'In the end, she simply repeats herself, softer and more sincere this time.'}
{3: 'But it\'s not the sky that\'s beautiful, not the sea of clouds turned pink and purple. It\'s her gentle eyes.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'It\'s the sound of a military drum—a rhythm that exists in neither reality nor simulation. In her daze, Teddy sees that strange little plush figure drumming again.'}
{3: 'The rules that once bound Deerbear are gone.'}
{3: 'She might never appear again, yet she has never truly vanished.'}

{1: '42', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=40>Science has tried to explain emotions a thousand times.\nThe human body is just a mess of chemical reactions.</size></color>', 3: '800', 4: '50', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{3: 'Science has tried to explain emotions a thousand times. The human body is just a mess of chemical reactions.'}
{1: '43', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=40>But what I feel now has nothing to do with that.</size></color>', 3: '800', 4: '-150', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{3: 'But what I feel now has nothing to do with that.'}
{1: '51', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=38>Love isn\'t a rush of hormones, or the haze of dopamine.</size></color>', 3: '90', 4: '300', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{1: '53', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=38>It\'s not a broken heart begging to be healed,</size></color>', 3: '90', 4: '-80', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{1: '54', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=38>or a missing piece waiting to be filled.</size></color>', 3: '1160', 4: '-220', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{3: 'Love isn\'t a rush of hormones, or the haze of dopamine. It isn\'t dissection or analysis. It\'s not a broken heart begging to be healed, or a missing piece waiting to be filled.', 4: '1'}
{1: '62', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=38>but you accept me as I am</size></color>', 3: '1160', 4: '200', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{1: '64', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=38>and let me stand by your side.</size></color>', 3: '1200', 4: '-220', 9: '3', 10: '1', 11: '1'}
{3: 'In this moment, I may be incomplete, but you accept me as I am and let me stand by your side.', 4: '1'}

{1: '71', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=48>Love is the act of embracing the unmeasurable, the unknowable, and the unreachable within an otherwise ordered world.</size></color>', 3: '30', 4: '50', 9: '3'}
{3: 'Love is the act of embracing the unmeasurable, the unknowable, and the unreachable within an otherwise ordered world.', 4: '1'}
{1: '71', 2: '<color=#ffffffff><size=60>Commandant, from now on... you\'re my Planck time.</size></color>', 4: '50', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{3: 'Commandant, from now on... you\'re my Planck time.', 4: '1'}
{3: 'I allow myself to fall, to lose control.'}
{3: 'It\'s a shame you\'ll never know about my tiny act of courage.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'It\'s nothing. Ah... look at that cloud. Doesn\'t it look like a deer?', 4: '1', 18: '100014382'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'That one, right there. It looks just like...', 4: '1', 18: '100014383'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Deerbear', 3: 'Just letting you feel what\'s in my heart. As your alter ego, I can\'t hide anything from you, and the same goes for you too.', 4: '1', 18: '100014384'}
{1: '1', 2: 'Teddy', 3: '(The deer... my heart... so that\'s it. I can\'t believe I\'m only realizing this now.)', 4: '1', 18: '100014385'}
{3: 'She gently places her hand over the place where her heart should be.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'How strange. It\'s such a beautiful sight, but I still feel like crying.', 4: '1', 18: '100014386'}
{3: 'Tears are the tides of the soul.'}
{1: '1', 3: 'She gazes at you, unable to look away, maybe never able to again.'}
{3: 'A soft rhythm rises—thump, thump-thump—the steady beat of a heart finding its way back.'}
{2: 'Teddy', 3: 'Commandant... that song. I finally know how to sing it.', 4: '1', 18: '100014387'}
{3: 'The song that was never written, yet always echoed in her heart.'}
{1: 'At this moment, she dedicates it to you.', 3: '45'}
{3: 'At this moment, she dedicates it to you.'}
{1: '92', 2: 'I\'ll be bothering you for a while...', 3: '-420', 4: '230', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{2: 'Oh, hi, Commandant. I\'ll be bothering you for a while, so please cooperate...'}
{1: '92', 2: 'You have to listen to music with Teddy, have tea with Teddy,', 3: '-320', 4: '58.6', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{2: 'Today\'s agenda is quite elaborate. You have to listen to music with Teddy, have tea with Teddy, and build the new building block sets with Teddy...'}
{1: '93', 2: 'and build the new building block sets with Teddy...', 3: '70', 4: '-200', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '91', 2: 'I\'m missing the concert, not visiting a single attraction,', 3: '400', 4: '360', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '92', 2: 'and I\'m basically using my vacation time to work...', 3: '-530', 4: '95', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{2: 'I\'m missing the concert, not visiting a single attraction, and I\'m basically using my vacation time to work... But I\'m not saying I\'m unhappy today.'}
{1: '93', 2: 'But I\'m not saying I\'m unhappy today.', 3: '550', 4: '-125', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '91', 2: 'Anyway, that\'s the deal. I may say no, but you must come and ask for my help.', 3: '440', 4: '350', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '92', 2: 'This is your special treatment. Others would kill for it.', 3: '318', 4: '43', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{2: 'Anyway, that\'s the deal. I may say no, but you must come and ask for my help. This is your special treatment. Others would kill for it. So go on, lean on me a little more, okay?'}
{1: '93', 2: 'So go on, lean on me a little more, okay?', 3: '179', 4: '-258', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '92', 2: 'Here, let me do some free work for you to make it up to you. \nHow does that sound?', 3: '450', 4: '80', 9: '3', 11: '1'}

{2: 'Hey, c\'mon, don\'t be mad. Here, let me do some free work for you to make it up to you. How does that sound? Think I\'m probably okay doing free work twice... or maybe three times at most. But that\'s as many times as I can take it!'}
{1: '93', 2: 'Think I\'m probably okay doing free work twice... or maybe three times at most. \nBut that\'s as many times as I can take it!', 3: '-300', 4: '-220', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '91', 2: 'I went to the store early\nfor baking powder and frosting,', 3: '593', 4: '362', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{1: '92', 2: 'and even asked my annoying brother for help,', 3: '-503', 4: '31.5', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{2: 'I went to the store early for baking powder and frosting, and even asked my annoying brother for help, just to get fresh cherries from the conservation area...'}
{1: '93', 2: 'just to get fresh cherries from the conservation area...', 3: '480', 4: '-235', 9: '3', 11: '1'}
{2: 'what I really wanted was to use this song to say something. Something I didn\'t quite know how to say aloud...'}
{1: 'what I really wanted was to use this song to say something. Something I didn\'t quite know how to say aloud...', 3: '45'}
{2: 'I\'ve already found the highest-rarity "treasure." ...No, not in a game, dummy.'}
